behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE push this to System: rename detection semantics come from real git behavior (e.g., rename detection thresholds/status formats), and a stubbed diff can mask missing flags or subprocess-output edge cases. Not Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is purely git/CLI logic with no AI step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert the report shows the file only in renamed (not added/deleted).

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real subprocess/output handling: binary diffs often trigger encoding/format behavior only visible with real git + real files. A mock text diff can always pass and hide decode/crash bugs (MOCK-HIDE). Not Agentic/Workflow: no model-dependent correctness.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI as a subprocess, assert exit code is success and binary-file counts are present in output.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on actual git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (REAL-INFRA). Mocking repository responses can hide real exit-code/stderr handling failures. Rejected higher levels: issue text describes a Python git-summary CLI only, with no AI/model steps.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with zero commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert it exits cleanly and prints an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim against real repository history, so the minimum boundary is real git repo + CLI execution. In-process fakes can miss history-walk, diff-range, and subprocess parsing mismatches (MOCK-HIDE). Not Agentic/Workflow: no LLM/ML output quality involved.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run CLI subprocess, and assert reported counts equal the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/worktree behavior at OS+git boundary, best caught with real repo state transitions and CLI subprocess. Mocked repository states may hide command-selection or diff-base bugs. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run CLI subprocess for each case, and assert outputs are byte-identical.
